title: willughbeia oblonga
intro: Willughbeia oblonga, also known as the Beccari's willughbeia, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, native to Papua New Guinea. It is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of a toxic compound called willughbein, which has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and its health benefits include reducing pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, Willughbeia oblonga is often used in the form of a tincture or decoction, and it is also sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
